text.title: cqrs-pattern Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the cqrs-pattern methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: A handler class is either a CommandHandler (returns None or ID) or a QueryHandler (returns a read model) — never both.
rule.rationale: Mixed handlers re-introduce the very coupling CQRS exists to break.

rule.statement: Commands return None or a single identifier; queries never mutate state. Mutation in a QueryHandler is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Returning rich state from commands leaks read-side concerns into the write side.

rule.statement: The write side enforces invariants on a rich domain model; the read side reads flat projections optimised per query.
rule.rationale: Separating the two lets each evolve independently and use the right storage.

rule.statement: Eventual consistency between sides is an explicit design choice; lag must be measured and documented (e.g., projection_lag_ms_p95).
rule.rationale: Hidden lag becomes a stale-read incident.

rule.statement: Use a Mediator (or DI container) for dispatch; handlers do not import each other directly.
rule.rationale: Direct cross-handler imports re-create the coupling CQRS removes.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
